expressions.korean: 깜빡 잊다
expressions.english:
to completely forget (for a short period of time)
to just forget; to slip one's mind

expressions.korean: 잊어버리다
expressions.english: to forget

expressions.korean: 약속을 잊어버리다
expressions.english: to forget an appointment

expressions.korean: 까망게 잊어버리다
expressions.english: to completely forget

expressions.korean: 시간 가는 것을 잊다
expressions.english: to forget the time passing
expressions.notes: 시간(이) 가다 = time passes; time goes

expressions.korean: 저럼 한 약속 잊으셨어요?
expressions.english: Did you forget the promise you made me?
expressions.notes: 약속을 하다 = to promise, make a promise

expressions.korean: 그 일을 잊을 수가 없어요.
expressions.english: I cannot forget about that incident.
expressions.notes:
일 = work; task; thing; stuff; occasion
-ㄹ(으)ㄹ 수가 없다 = to be not able to + V, can't

expressions.korean: 여권 챙기는 거 잊지 마세요.
expressions.english:
Don't forget to take your passport.
Don't forget to pack your passport.
expressions.notes:
여권 = passport
챙기다 = to take care; to take; to pack

expressions.korean: 윗지 말고 우유 좀 사다 주세요.
expressions.english: Please do not forget to buy and bring me some milk.
expressions.notes:
우유 = milk
좀 = a little; please
사다 = to buy
주세요 = to buy something for someone that person

expressions.korean: 이미 지난 일이니 잊어버리세요.
expressions.english: It's a past incident, so forget about it.

expressions.korean: 그때 한 약속 벌써 잊은 거예요?
expressions.english: Did you already forget the promise you made back then?
expressions.notes: 그때 = at that time; back then

expressions.korean: 그 일을 어떻게 잊을 수가 있겠어요.
expressions.english: How can I forget that incident?
expressions.notes:
어떻게 = how
-ㄹ(으)ㄹ 수가 있다 = can, to be able to; there is a good chance that + S + V

expressions.korean: 매일 아침 꽃에 물 주는 것 잊지 마세요.
expressions.english: Don't forget to water the flowers every morning.
expressions.notes:
매일 = every day
아침 = morning; breakfast
꽃 = flower
물 주다 = to water; to give water

expressions.korean: 어쨌게 제 생일을 잊어버릴 수가 있어요?
expressions.english: How could you forget my birthday?

expressions.korean: 전화 드리기로 하고 깜빡 잊었네요, 죄송해요.
expressions.english: I said I would give you a call and it slipped my mind. I'm sorry.
expressions.notes:
전화 = phone call; telephone
전화 드리다 = (polite) to give one a call, to call
-기로 하다 = to plan to + V; to decide to + V; to make a promise to + V
-네요 = is a sentence ending that expresses realization of a fact or agreement to a statement.
죄송해요. = (polite) I'm sorry.

expressions.korean: 너무 바빠서 밥 먹는 것도 잊어버리고 있었어요.
expressions.english: I was so busy that I forgot to eat.
expressions.notes:
너무 = too much, excessively; very
바쁘다 = to be busy
-이/이/여서 = because, since
밥 = food; meal; cooked rice
먹다 = to eat; to drink
-도 = too, also

expressions.korean: 비밀번호를 잊어버려서 로그인을 못 하고 있어요.
expressions.english: I forgot my password, so I am unable to log-in.
expressions.notes:
비밀번호 = password
못 = can't
로그인 = log-in
로그인을 하다 = to log-in

expressions.korean: 친구랑 만나기로 약속하고는 까망게 잊어버렸어요.
expressions.english: I made a plan (to meet up) with my friend and I completely forgot.
expressions.notes:
친구 = friend
만나다 = to meet (up)
-기로 약속하다 = to promise to + V

expressions.korean: 오늘 아침에 중요한 회의가 있다는 걸 잊고 있었어요.
expressions.english: I forgot that there was an important meeting this morning.
expressions.notes:
오늘 = today
중요하다 = to be important
중요한 = is the adjective form of 중요하다.
회의 = meeting
있다 = to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.korean: 친구랑 오랜만에 만나서 시간 가는 것도 잊고 놀았어요.
expressions.english: I hung out with my friend and we ended up losing track of time.
expressions.notes:
오랜만에 = first time in a long period of time
놀다 = to play; to hang out
